title: Ophrys hebes
taxonomy: Ophrys hebes is a species of orchid in the genus Ophrys. The genus Ophrys, to which Ophrys hebes belongs, is a group of orchids commonly known as bee orchids. These orchids are native to the Mediterranean region and are characterized by their unique flowers that mimic the appearance of female insects. Ophrys hebes is a type of bee orchid that is found in Spain and Portugal. It is a terrestrial orchid that grows in well-drained areas and is often associated with limestone rocks.

intro: Ophrys hebes, also known as Hebe Orchid or False Bee Orchid, is a type of orchid that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Ophrys hebes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ophrys hebes is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs in remedies for respiratory and digestive issues.
